---
title: "PyApprox UQ Tutorials"
---

## Welcome

Learn uncertainty quantification with PyApprox through hands-on tutorials and structured workshops.

## Quick Links

::::: {.grid}

:::: {.g-col-6}
### [Tutorial Library](library/index.qmd)

Browse all tutorials organized by topic:

- Forward UQ & Monte Carlo
- Sensitivity Analysis
- Bayesian Inference
- Surrogate Modeling
- Multifidelity Methods
::::

:::: {.g-col-6}
### [Workshops](workshops/index.qmd)

Structured learning paths for specific events:

- Introduction to UQ (4 hours)
- More workshops coming soon
::::

:::::

## Getting Started

New to PyApprox? Follow these steps:

1. **[Setup Environment](in_progress/setup_environment.qmd)** - Install PyApprox and verify your setup
2. **[UQ Problem Framing](in_progress/uq_problem_framing.qmd)** - Learn the core concepts
3. **[Monte Carlo Basics](in_progress/monte_carlo_basics.qmd)** - Your first UQ analysis

## About

PyApprox is a Python library for uncertainty quantification developed at Sandia National Laboratories. These tutorials cover the modern `pyapprox.typing` module, which provides:

- Backend-agnostic code (NumPy/PyTorch)
- Type-safe interfaces
- Comprehensive UQ workflows

## Resources

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/sandialabs/pyapprox)
- [Documentation](https://sandialabs.github.io/pyapprox/)
- [Issues & Support](https://github.com/sandialabs/pyapprox/issues)
